calendar.matters: FUTURE ACCOUNTING

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Oct-21-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Lillian Ng for successor trustee Andrea Leung (via Zoom).
Zachary McCoy for settlor and conservatee Virginia Gualberto (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on First Account of Successor Trustee, Petition for Approval of Allowance to Settlor, for Approval and Allowance of Fees and Costs of Successor Trustee and Attorney for Successor Trustee and for Increase of Bond.

McCoy requested that the scope of his legal representation be expanded to include representation in the trust case. The Court approved the request. Mr. McCoy indicated that he would prepare a proposed order and have it submitted later in the day.

Mr. McCoy further reported that he had spoken with Ms. Gualberto regarding the petition and that she understood and did not object to the requests. She was agreeable to the fees sought by both Ms. Leung and Ms. Ng and was pleased that she would continue to receive a personal allowance.

Following discussion, the Court took the matter under submission with the intent to grant as prayed.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Appearances: Lillian Ng appeared by zoom for and with Andrea Leung by zoom.
Christopher Jew (court appointed counsel) for and with Virginia Gualberto.

Discussion was held regarding the tax lien still being in negotiations, Mrs. Gualberto's monthly income and how the amount varies, the $64,000.00 in HOA fees that are owed and accruing, the unit not being able to be rented out because of the HOA's position that they will not allow anyone to benefit from the property until the fees are paid, the HOA threatening with foreclosure and being aggressive about it, the need to sell the property in order to pay the outstanding fees, Mrs. Gualberto's residence being worth about 2.5 million and the possibility of taking out a line of credit, Mrs. Gualberto's assets and income not being enough to keep up with the expenses, the proposed sale amount of $600,000.00 being based the current market, the property not being so appealing because of the sinking issue and the lack of a view as it is on the 6th floor versus the 30th floor, the outstanding bills being close to $200,000.00 (with the HOA fees and taxes being $100,000.00), the trust account being barely at $30,000.00, the need to repair the roof at Topaz Way rather than replacing it due to lack of funds, the home owner insurance not renewing because the roof was not replaced, a $3,400.00 monthly rental income estimate, the court's position that the petition is not well supported at this time, there already being a mortgage on the Topaz Way property Mrs. Ng's understanding that a loan is not an option, etc. The Petition for Authority to Sell Settlor's Real Property, etc. is denied.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Magdaleno. Not reported.

Petition For Instructions Confirming Identification Of Trustee Filling Vacancy In The Office Of Trustee, And Authorizing Her To Continue To Serve With
Off Calendar Jun-14-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Emilie Calhoun, in court, for and with Gerardine Gualberto Duschane, via Zoom, Petitioner; Lillian Ng, via Zoom, for and with Andrea Leung, Conservator; Amy Harrington, via Zoom, court-appointed counsel for Virginia Gualberto, Conservatee.

The court confirms with Ms. Harrington that she believed that her client would appear today and advises that her client's position has not changed regarding not wanting her daughter to serve as successor trustee. Ms. Calhoun reports the real property is vacant and that the conservator had been paying the expenses related to the property but ceased doing so this past January and recognizes the strained relationship between her client and her client's mother. Ms. Harrington offers testimony regarding the real property and recommends that it be placed on the market for rent or to sell. The court offers it's tentative ruling to take the Petition for Instructions under submission and continue the Status of Accounting in the Conservatorship matter. No objections to the court's tentative ruling are presented.

The Petition for Instructions, etc., is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Bastain. Not Reported.
